title: Milar Travis

'''Milar Travis'''<ref name="TEGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> was a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Droids 8" /> By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Travis, alongside fellow Rebel [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] [[Fin Danglot]] and [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], was [[Teacher/Legends|mentored]] by the pilot [[Puck Naeco/Legends|Puck Naeco]].<ref name="TEGW" /> In 0 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> Travis was stationed at the [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]], the Alliance's base on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].<ref name="Droids 8" />

Prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="Droids 8" /> she<ref name="Warfare" /> attended a briefing held by the base's [[Commander/Legends|commander]], [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]], who discussed the Alliance's plans to attack the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|DS-1 Orbital Battle Station]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], arranging for the base's starfighter [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] to carry out the [[Space warfare/Legends|attack]]. Travis was a fair-skinned [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="Droids 8" /> [[Gender/Legends|female]]<ref name="Warfare" /> with [[Color/Legends|blonde]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] who sported [[Piercing/Legends|earrings]]. She wore the standard [[Rebel flight suit]], with a name tape displaying her surname in the [[High Galactic/Legends|High Galactic alphabet]] on her [[uniform]].<ref name="Droids 8" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|<nowiki />''[…]'' Red 12 finally gets a name, chosen after sorting through a number of candidates. They were Naeco (original ''X-wing'' game), Captain Ernek Marskan (same), Fin Danglot (''Galaxy Guide 1''), Travis (a blonde woman from Marvel's retelling of ''A New Hope'' back in the ''Droids'' kid's comic – how's ''that'' for obscure?), and Talos Merkin (''Captive to Evil'').|Jason Fry, ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'' endnotes|<ref name="Warfare" />}}
Milar Travis was introduced in the [[Droids (1986) 8|eighth issue]] of [[Marvel Comics]]' [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book series]], written by [[David Manak]], illustrated by [[Ernie Colón]],<ref name="Droids 8" /> and published on [[March 17]], [[1987]].<ref name="Age">{{BookCite|title=[[Marvel Age]]|number=Vol. 1 No. 50|publisher=[[Marvel Comics]] Group|date=[[May]] [[1987]]|issn=8750-4367}}</ref> Later, [[Jason Fry]] would consider Travis as one of several candidates to [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] as the previously unnamed role of [[Red Twelve/Legends|Red Twelve]] during the Battle of Yavin for the [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', before ultimately settling on Puck Naeco. Nonetheless, both Travis and Fin Danglot were separately included in the book as a shoutout. Travis was also given the first name of "Milar" in that source, a [[tuckerization]] of ''[[Star Wars]]'' authors [[Karen Miller]] and [[Karen Traviss]].<ref name="Warfare" />
